title: SQL vs NoSQL: Explained by the Griffins!

dialogue.line: Stewie, what's the deal with SQL and NoSQL? Sounds like mumbo jumbo!
Ah, Peter, think of SQL as a well-organized library.
While NoSQL is more like a messy yet cozy bookstore.
SQL databases use tables to store data in a structured way.
NoSQL databases store data in flexible formats like JSON or documents.
So, it's like tidy spreadsheets vs. free-form notebooks?
Precisely, Peter! Use SQL for complex queries, NoSQL for dynamic growth.
Right, like when Lois organizes my socks. Structured!
And NoSQL is your sock drawer on laundry day. Chaos!
Got it! My socks are like data, sometimes neatly paired, sometimes... not.
Exactly! Now if only your brain had a SQL database.
